description_long: This fence is constructed from a series of vertically aligned, sharpened wooden stakes, each with a pointed tip, giving it a defensive and rustic appearance. The stakes are uneven in height, creating an irregular, jagged top edge, and are reinforced by two horizontal rails that zigzag across the front, providing structural support. The wood appears to be unfinished, with visible grain and natural color variations, suggesting it is made from raw timber. The fence is supported by thicker vertical posts at intervals, and the rails are attached in a way that mimics a hand-built, utilitarian style. There are no visible decorative elements or gates, and the construction is solid, likely intended to serve as a barrier or fortification. The overall look is robust and functional, suitable for use in parks, gardens, or as a temporary urban barrier.
description: A rugged wooden fence made of pointed vertical stakes and zigzagging horizontal rails, with a natural, unfinished timber appearance. The top edge is irregular and jagged, emphasizing its defensive and rustic style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed stakes, irregular top edge, and two zigzagging horizontal rails for reinforcement.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the protruding horizontal rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the vertical stakes and the back of the horizontal rails, with less detail visible than the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the arrangement of the horizontal rails.
